summary: DHH questions how domain registrar Speednames can charge over 400% markup on domain names compared to competitors like Buy Domains. He initially couldn't understand why anyone would pay $85 for a two-year lease when the same thing costs $20 elsewhere. After personally experiencing the painful process of switching domain providers, he discovered the answer: switching costs create lock-in that sustains the markup.
key_points: Speednames charges $85 for domain leases that competitors sell for around $20, a 400%+ markup
High switching costs between domain providers create effective lock-in that sustains inflated pricing
DHH learned firsthand how excruciating the provider migration process is
money_quotes: I wondered how a company like Speednames could be successful selling domain names at a 400%+ mark-up.
That was before I knew what an excruciating process it is to switch service provider.
But I learned. Oh, I learned.
key_insight: Companies can sustain massive price premiums on commodity products when switching costs are high enough to lock customers in.
